sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
The calculation of reload time using reload speed is: Reload Time = Base Reload Time ÷ (1 + Reload Speed Bonus)
At max rank, a +30% reload speed bonus reduces reload times by about 23%.
On Bow type weapons such as the  Paris, Fast Hands increases the notching speed of the arrow.

Notes
The calculation of reload time using reload speed is: Reload Time = Base Reload Time ÷ (1 + Reload Speed Bonus)
At max rank, a +30% reload speed bonus reduces reload times by about 23%.
On Bow type weapons such as the  Paris, Fast Hands increases the notching speed of the arrow.

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
Though dropped infrequently, it can be obtained as a reward from Dark Sector defense missions.
There is a relatively easy way to farm this on the Kuva Fortress tile set. In many end rooms, there are Latcher Turrets that will deploy 2 waves of 8 Latchers when your warframe is detected by the scanners. An AoE weapon such as  Ignis or a weapon that chains like  Atomos can make quick work of the Latchers and you can repeat this infinitely. The turret will retreat into the ground after deploying its second wave, you can spawn it again by being scanned another time.

sections.text:

Does not affect Incarnon mode.

sections.title: Drop Locations
sections.text: An unranked Serration is given upon completion of The Teacher Quest.

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
Serration damage is applied before any other damage type. Therefore, every other damage increasing mod benefits from Serration. E.g. a weapon with a base damage of 18 equipped with a rank 9 Serration mod (150%) would have a new base damage of 45: 18 × (1 + 1.5) = 45
This new base damage will be what is used to calculate elemental damages.
When paired with other mods:
Multiplicatively stacks with Faction Damage Mods for a maximum of +244.5% damage.
+310.75% for Primed Faction Damage Mods.
Additively stacks with  Heavy Caliber for a maximum of +330% damage.
Stacks with  Heavy Caliber and Faction Damage Mods for a total of +459% damage at max rank.
+566.5% for Primed Faction Damage Mods.
At higher ranks the fusion cost of this mod becomes quite costly. Upgrading other mods simultaneously makes for a better investment of your  Endo compared to only enhancing this one alone.
It is suggested to upgrade this mod to at least rank 5 quickly though, since it is useful on a lot of weapons.
Maxing this mod requires 20,460  Endo and 988,218 Credits 988,218.

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
Can be combined with  Vile Acceleration,  Spring-Loaded Chamber,  Vigilante Fervor and  Primed Shred to provide a 330% (660% for Bows) increase in fire rate while aiming, otherwise 255% (510% for bows)
Can decrease the delay on Chargeable weapons such as  Dread,  Paris Prime,  Lanka,  Miter and  Ogris.
Has no visual effect on continuous weapons (e.g. the  Flux Rifle and  Ignis) as they shoot out a continuous beam. The number of damage instances will increase with higher fire rate.

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
Has no effect on  Corrupted and  Narmer Corpus (e.g.  Corrupted Crewman,  Narmer Crewman).
Despite being listed as a multiplier, it will stack additively with other sources of Damage to Corpus.
For example, if you had a Riven Mod with x0.5 Damage to Corpus and a max rank Expel Corpus with x1.3 Damage to Corpus, you would have a 1 - 0.5 + 0.3 = x0.8 multiplier for damage against Corpus with both mods equipped.

sections.title: Drop Locations
sections.text: An unranked Hornet Strike is given upon completion of The Teacher Quest.

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
Base damage bonuses are multiplied against the base damage of the weapon.
For example, a weapon with a base damage of 100 would deal 320 damage when modded with a max-rank Hornet Strike equipped: 100 × (1 + 2.2) = 320.
Base damage bonuses are also multiplied against added elemental damage.
For example, a weapon with a base damage of 100, modded with  Deep Freeze would deal 190 damage. Adding a max-rank Hornet Strike would apply a 220% damage bonus to both the base and elemental damage, resulting in 608 damage with both mods installed: 100 × (1 + 0.9) × (1 + 2.2) = 608.
The high effect of Hornet Strike when combined with elemental damage mods makes it a frequent choice for secondary weapons where the goal is to deal as much damage as possible. Hornet Strike is available from the nearly the beginning of the game, making it a reliable mod choice and a good investment of  Endo during the Star Chart section of the game.
Although larger buffs (like those created by  Cascadia Flare and  Galvanized Shot) can outperform Hornet Strike, those won't be available until significant progress into the later portion of the game has been made, and operate via more involved mechanics.
When more than one damage bonus is present, they are merely added together, not multiplied.
For example, a weapon with a base damage of 100 with a max-rank Hornet Strike equipped would deal only a small amount more when  Pistol Amp (+27% Pistol Damage) is equipped: 100 × (1 + 2.2 + 0.27) = 347.

sections.text:
Cannot be equiped with  Creeping Bullseye.
Pistol Gambit is additive with similar mods. For example, a  Sicarus with a max rank Pistol Gambit and  Hydraulic Crosshairs will have a 16% × (1 + 120% + 135%) = 56.8% critical chance.

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
The calculation of reload time using reload speed is: Reload Time = Base Reload Time ÷ (1 + Reload Speed Bonus)
At max rank, expect a 33% reduction to reload times. 1/(1 + 0.48) ~ (2/3 of original reload speed)
Stacking this with  Stunning Speed should reduce reload times by 47% (or 53% of base reload speed)

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
For any non-integer magazine values the magazine size is simply rounded to the nearest whole number. For example, a  Furis modded with a max rank Slip Magazine will have a calculated magazine size of 35 × (1 + 30%) = 45.5, which will simply round to 45.5.
Is completely inferior to  Ice Storm which can give a larger increase to Magazine Capacity (40%) and a 40% increase in Cold damage for the exact same mod capacity cost.

sections.text: For any non-integer maximum ammo capacity values the capacity is rounded down to the nearest whole number. For example, a  Kulstar modded with a max rank Trick Mag will have a calculated maximum ammo capacity of 15 × (1 + 90%) = 28.5, which will round down to 28.

excerpt:
Notes
For any non-integer maximum ammo capacity values the capacity is rounded down to the nearest whole number. For example, a  Kulstar modded with a max rank Trick Mag will have a calculated maximum ammo capacity of 15 × (1 + 90%) = 28.5, which will round down to 28.

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
For any non-integer magazine values the magazine size is simply rounded up to the nearest whole number. For example, a  Strun modded with a max rank Ammo Stock will have a calculated magazine size of 6 × (1 + 60%) = 9.6, which will simply round to 10.
A shotgun with only 1 base magazine capacity (such as  Exergis) will have 1 × (1 + 60%) = 1.6, rounded to 2 magazine capacity.

sections.title: Notes
sections.text:
Affects base damage which is applied before every other damage type. Therefore, every other damage increasing mod is benefiting from Point Blank as well.
This mod combined with  Vicious Spread and  Blaze give off a total damage increase of +240%.
With  Primed Point Blank instead of Point Blank, this adds up for a total damage increase of +315% with Vicious Spread and Blaze.
Due to how it affects the base damage, this mod is necessary for all shotgun weapons in order to use them optimally. Few mods can replace the effectiveness of this mod.

sections.text: For any non-integer maximum ammo capacity values the capacity is rounded down to the nearest whole number. For example, an  Astilla modded with a max rank Shell Compression will have a calculated maximum ammo capacity of 112 × (1 + 90%) = 212.8, which will round down to 212.
